names.scientific_name: Monocalcium phosphate (E341i), Dicalcium phosphate (E341ii), Tricalcium phosphate (E341iii)

classification.sub_class: raising agent, anti-caking agent, firming agent, calcium source

natural_sources: bones and teeth (as hydroxyapatite, a form of calcium phosphate)
natural_occurrence.description: Calcium phosphates are the main mineral component of bones and teeth. In nature, they exist primarily as hydroxyapatite. The forms used as food additives are synthetically produced for purity and consistency.

manufacturing.description: Produced by reacting phosphoric acid with calcium hydroxide, calcium carbonate, or calcium chloride. The molar ratio of reactants determines which form is produced: monocalcium phosphate (E341i, Ca(H2PO4)2), dicalcium phosphate (E341ii, CaHPO4), or tricalcium phosphate (E341iii, Ca3(PO4)2). Each form has different solubility and functional properties.

other_uses.cosmetics: Abrasive in toothpaste, opacifying agent, bulking agent.
other_uses.industrial: Animal feed supplements, fertilizers, polishing agents.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Calcium supplement, tablet excipient, antacid, toothpaste abrasive.

eu.conditions: Quantum satis in many categories. E341iii commonly used as anti-caking agent. Subject to overall phosphate intake monitoring.

usa.notes: Widely used in baking powder and as calcium fortification

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: 0-70 mg/kg bw/day (as phosphorus, for all phosphates)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: 40 mg/kg bw/day (as phosphorus, for all phosphates)

regulatory_history.event: EFSA reviewed all food phosphates and expressed concern about total dietary phosphate intake from additives. However, calcium phosphates are generally viewed more favorably due to dual benefit of providing calcium.

commonly_found_in: Baking powder (E341i - monocalcium phosphate is the acid component)
Powdered spices and seasonings (E341iii as anti-caking agent)
Toothpaste (as mild abrasive and calcium source)
fun_facts: Monocalcium phosphate is the key acidic component in most commercial baking powders - it reacts with baking soda to produce carbon dioxide for leavening
Your bones and teeth are made primarily of a natural form of calcium phosphate called hydroxyapatite
Tricalcium phosphate is used as an anti-caking agent to keep salt, spices, and powdered sugar free-flowing
Serves a dual purpose: provides both calcium supplementation and phosphate functionality
Dicalcium phosphate is a common calcium supplement in both human nutrition and animal feed
